page: Amazon Web Services c7gn.4xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an AWS Graviton ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 processor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores with 16 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz with no multi-threading. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong memory write capabilities, with large-block uncached memory write bandwidth reaching 96.92 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25 percent of tested hardware. The PassMark memory score is in the top 10 percent at 2953.84. However, cryptographic performance is relatively weak, scoring 2229.14 in the PassMark CPU encryption test. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance metrics, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, fall within the average range. Network capabilities are defined by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture provides a high density of dedicated physical cores relative to memory, making it a viable option for network-heavy and compute-bound workloads that do not require local SSD storage. The high baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps and strong memory write performance make this instance suitable for network appliances, data compression tasks, static web serving, and distributed compute workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.4xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server instance powered by a 2.6 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores, 16 vCPUs, and 32.0 GB of memory, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance is optimized for network and EBS performance, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong memory write bandwidth of 96.92 GB/sec and a top-tier PassMark memory score, though cryptographic performance is weaker. This instance is designed for network-intensive applications, static web serving, and compute-bound workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.4xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 32 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for network-heavy and compute workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.4xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 32 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores, high-speed memory write bandwidth, and 50 Gbps baseline network performance.
bullet_points: Powered by 16 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 ARM64 cores
Equipped with 32.0 GB of memory and no local storage
Delivers 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput workloads
Strong memory write performance and top-tier PassMark memory score
Suited for network appliances, static web serving, and compute-bound tasks
